degradation_rate: Surface granule loss of 15-25% after 5,000 hours xenon arc exposure per ASTM G155 Cycle 1. Underlying bitumen shows initial surface oxidation and micro-cracking at 3,000 hours. Elongation retention 70% of original value after 5,000 hours exposure.
service_life_impact: Granule loss exposes underlying modified bitumen to accelerated UV degradation. Industry estimates granule-surfaced SBS modified bitumen cap sheets maintain UV protection for 15-25 years depending on UV intensity and granule embedment quality. Once granule coverage drops below 60%, degradation rate accelerates significantly.

climate_context.key_stressor: UV radiation — cumulative dose increases with latitude-adjusted solar exposure. Higher severity in zones 1-3 (southern US) than zones 5-7 (northern US).

notes: SBS (styrene-butadiene-styrene) modified bitumen cap sheet with ceramic granule surface. Granule color affects UV absorption — darker granules absorb more heat but UV degradation mechanism is primarily photochemical oxidation of the bitumen binder. White or light-colored granules reduce surface temperature but UV exposure remains. Data from accelerated weathering per ASTM G155; natural weathering studies show similar degradation patterns at approximately 1:8 acceleration ratio.
